goals.g1: Can Compound Match Resolution reuse each rule's precomputed schema outputs (SchemaCore/EditsArtifact/FragmentArtifact) for an overlap zone, or must a new/merged transition graph be authored and translated per overlap zone?

c1.statement: SchemaCore.inherit/.born/.boundary_grab/.internal_edges are compiled ONCE from a single rule's OWN graph2, with no reference to any other rule's graph2 or bindings

c2.statement: the compiled artifacts are binding-independent: the same SchemaCore/EditsArtifact/FragmentArtifact is reused across every firing of that rule; only the binding dict varies per fire

c3.statement: _read_boundary_externals reads a rule's boundary_grab crossings LIVE before that rule's own teardown, with no knowledge of whether another rule will also transform the outside node

c4.statement: rebuild() strips ALL edges off its own surviving real nodes unconditionally, then rebuilds only its own internal_edges plus its own saved boundary crossings

c5.statement: compile_core's inherit-claim loop is injective only within one rule's own output_nodes, with no arbitration against a different rule mapping the same real node

c6.statement: Compound Match Resolution's boundary_of_one_inside_other case: a boundary node under rule A that is transformed by rule B

c7.statement: Compound Match Resolution's overlapping_nodes case: a real node matched as an input by both rule A and rule B

c8.statement: naive sequential single-rule apply drops rule A's crossing edge when rule B later strips the shared node's edges, since B's compiled schema has no entry for A's edge

c9.statement: naive sequential apply lets two rules' inherit decisions collide on the same real id; the framework requires one to be forced-born, a decision neither compiled artifact encodes alone

c10.statement: the KB framework frames the mechanism as an extension of the current triple formalism, not a new substrate requirement, and never describes generating a new pattern/graph2 per overlap

c11.statement: both rebuild() and layer_apply_schema() already compute output_map: schema output Node -> real Node purely from that rule's own binding+inherit/born (schema.py 366-407, 505-516)

c12.statement: DECISION: precompiled per-rule schema artifacts are REUSED unmodified; a new apply-time compound orchestrator sits above them -- collects all boundary externals across the whole firing set before any teardown, resolves inherit-collisions and crossing-redirections using each rule's own output_map, then writes the combined layer once. No new/authored transition graph (graph2) is needed per overlap zone; the fix lives entirely in apply-time orchestration, not pattern authoring.

derived_from.rule: reuse-plus-orchestrator resolves both concrete failure modes (c8,c9) using information already available per-rule (c11), consistent with the framework's own stated formalism (c10)

ans_reuse.statement: Reuse precompiled artifacts + new apply-time compound orchestrator; no new graph2 per overlap.

ans_new_graph.statement: Author/translate a new merged graph2 per overlap zone.

k1.method: grep schema.py for output_map computation sites
k1.result: confirmed: rebuild() computes output_map at 366-407, layer_apply_schema() at 505-516, both purely from binding+inherit/born -- no new pattern object needed

k2.result: c12's shape (routing/timing/arbitration layer) matches c10's description of the fix as routing/threading, not new-pattern generation

k3.method: o2 check c12 resolves c8 and c9 concretely
k3.result: c8 resolved by reading all boundary externals up front across the whole firing set before any rule's edge-strip, redirecting via the transforming rule's own output_map. c9 resolved by an explicit forced-born override decided by the ranked overlap algorithm before either rule's identity-resolution runs. Both are apply-time changes only.

obligations.o2: must actually resolve c8 and c9, not merely gesture at them

committed.statement: DECISION: precompiled per-rule schema artifacts are REUSED unmodified; a new apply-time compound orchestrator sits above them -- collects all boundary externals across the whole firing set before any teardown, resolves inherit-collisions and crossing-redirections using each rule's own output_map, then writes the combined layer once. No new/authored transition graph (graph2) is needed per overlap zone; the fix lives entirely in apply-time orchestration, not pattern authoring.
